message:
perf(raptorq): Flat row-major Gaussian elimination + linalg scale_add_assign

Rewrites the dense Gaussian elimination phase in InactivationDecoder
to use a flat row-major Vec<Gf256> instead of Vec<Vec<Gf256>>:

Decoder (both standard and proof-traced variants):
- Matrix layout: vec![vec![Gf256; n_cols]; n_rows] → vec![Gf256; n_rows * n_cols]
  Eliminates per-row heap allocation, improves cache locality during elimination
- Pivot buffers: Pre-allocate single pivot_buf and pivot_rhs vectors, copy
  pivot row data via copy_from_slice instead of cloning per iteration
- Solution extraction: Use std::mem::take to move RHS vectors instead of
  cloning, avoiding allocation for each solved symbol
- Solution computation: Use std::mem::take for rhs[eq_idx] in both
  solve methods (standard + proof-traced)

Linalg:
- Add SparseRow::scale_add_assign() for in-place scale-add without
  allocating an intermediate scaled copy. Sorted merge with zero removal.

GF256/tests:
- Remove unnecessary .clone() on original vector (clippy suggestion)
- Remove unnecessary .clone() on constraints vector in fuzz tests

Encoding:
- Fix source symbol zero-padding: explicitly fill buffer with zeros before
  partial copy to prevent stale pool data from leaking into short symbols
- Add test_source_symbol_zero_pads_with_pool verifying the fix

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
